name: ExtensionComposition_Subject
title: R5: subject
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `Composition.subject` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`Composition.subject` 0..* `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Resource)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `Composition.subject` 0..* `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Resource)`
*  R4B: `Composition.subject` 0..1 `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Resource)`
*  R4: `Composition.subject` 0..1 `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Resource)`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `Composition.subject` allows multiple values in the source, but is mapped to an element that does not allow multiple values in the target.
Element `Composition.subject` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Composition.subject` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.

element.comment:
Element `Composition.subject` allows multiple values in the source, but is mapped to an element that does not allow multiple values in the target.
Element `Composition.subject` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Composition.subject` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
For clinical documents, this is usually the patient.

element.short: Who and/or what the composition is about
element.definition: Who or what the composition is about. The composition can be about a person, (patient or healthcare practitioner), a device (e.g. a machine) or even a group of subjects (such as a document about a herd of livestock, or a set of patients that share a common exposure).
element.comment: For clinical documents, this is usually the patient.
